key_facts: The US-Iran war began approximately late February/early March 2026, with CBS reporting the conflict's '100th day' on June 8, 2026.
US CENTCOM conducted multiple rounds of strikes on Iran in June 2026, with at least two consecutive nights of exchanges on June 10-11.
Iran fired back at Gulf states and Jordan in response to US strikes (AP News, June 11).
Kuwait closed its airspace during the US strikes on Iran (CNBC, June 10).
Trump threatened to strike Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal but called off a third night of strikes on June 11.
Defense Secretary Hegseth defended the strikes with the phrase 'negotiate with bombs' (Al Jazeera, June 10).
Iran said the ceasefire was 'practically meaningless' after US strikes (Euronews).
Pakistan's prime minister served as a key mediator and confirmed the deal wording was agreed upon (AP, June 13).
On June 14, a deal was reached to end the war and Trump ordered a stop to the US naval blockade (PBS).
On June 15, US and Iran announced an initial deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz (NPR, NBC, NYT).
The deal was reportedly set to be signed on Friday (CNBC, June 14).
Israeli strikes in Lebanon threatened the ceasefire agreement (CBS, June 15).
Israel felt 'sidelined' with its war aims 'unachieved' by the deal (Christian Science Monitor, June 15).
Netanyahu refrained from criticizing the Iran deal (DW, June 16).
Analysts described the deal as 'a costly return to prewar conditions' with unresolved nuclear questions (The Conversation, June 15).
US fuel prices were projected to take 'months' to normalize after the deal (Al Jazeera).
An Apache helicopter was reportedly shot down by Iran, prompting US retaliatory strikes (CBS, June 9).
Indian sailors were killed in the Strait of Hormuz during the conflict (Al Jazeera, June 12).
The Council on Foreign Relations published an analysis titled 'Trump's Iran Deal: What We Know, What's Contested, and What Remains Unresolved' on June 15.
The Institute for the Study of War published an 'Iran Update Special Report' on June 14-15.
